node.statement: EXACT E2b (LLL wavefunctions, symmetric gauge): A=-(1/2)r x B. LLL (n=0) states: phi_m(z) = z^m * exp(-|z|^2/4) / sqrt(2*pi*ell^2*2^m*m\!) where z=(x+iy)/ell, m=0,1,2,... Angular momentum L_z = hbar*m. Any analytic f(z)*exp(-|z|^2/4) is in LLL. KEY PROPERTY: analyticity constrains LLL states so severely that relative angular momentum m of a pair UNIQUELY determines the two-body state. [P11-Girvin eqs (1.76)-(1.77)]
